title: parsonsia llavea
intro: Parsonsia llavea, also known as the False Forget-me-not, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It is native to the Pacific region, particularly in New Zealand, Australia, and some parts of the Pacific Islands. Parsonsia llavea has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to utilize its health benefits, such as treating colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
